int.name: int
int.description:
Sequence of digits without commas or decimals and optional sign character (ASCII characters "-" and "0" - "9" ). The sign character utilizes one byte (i.e. positive int is "99999" while negative int is "-99999"). Note that int values may contain leading zeros (e.g. "00023" = "23").
Examples:
723 in field 21 would be mapped int as |21=723|.
-723 in field 12 would be mapped int as |12=-723|
The following data types are based on int.

float.name: float
float.description: Sequence of digits with optional decimal point and sign character (ASCII characters "-", "0" - "9" and "."); the absence of the decimal point within the string will be interpreted as the float representation of an integer value. All float fields must accommodate up to fifteen significant digits. The number of decimal places used should be a factor of business/market needs and mutual agreement between counterparties.

Qty.name: Qty
Qty.description: float field (see definition of "float" above) capable of storing either a whole number (no decimal places) of "shares" or a decimal value containing decimal places for non-share quantity asset classes.

Price.name: Price
Price.description: float field (see definition of "float" above) representing a price. Note the number of decimal places may vary.

PriceOffset.name: PriceOffset
PriceOffset.description: float field (see definition of "float" above) representing a price offset, which can be mathematically added to a "Price". Note the number of decimal places may vary and some fields such as LastForwardPoints may be negative.

Amt.name: Amt
Amt.description: float field typically representing a Price times a Qty

char.name: char
char.description: Single character value, can include any alphanumeric character or punctuation except the delimiter. All char fields are case sensitive

Boolean.name: Boolean
Boolean.description:
char field containing one of two values:
'Y' = True/Yes
'N' = False/No

String.name: String
String.description: Alpha-numeric free format strings, can include any character or punctuation except the delimiter. All String fields are case sensitive.

MultipleStringValue.name: MultipleStringValue
MultipleStringValue.description: String field containing one or more space delimited multiple character values.

Currency.name: Currency
Currency.description: String field (see definition of "String" above) representing a currency type (see Appendix A-Valid Currency Codes).

Exchange.name: Exchange
Exchange.description: String field (see definition of "String" above) representing a market or exchange. (see Appendix C-Reuters Exchange Mnemonics).

UTCTimestamp.name: UTCTimestamp
UTCTimestamp.description:
Time/date combination represented in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") in either YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS (whole seconds) or YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss (milliseconds) format, colons, dash, and period required.
Valid values:
* YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31, HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-59 (without milliseconds).
* YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31, HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-59. sss=000-999 (indicating milliseconds).

UTCTimeOnly.name: UTCTimeOnly
UTCTimeOnly.description:
Time-only represented in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") in either HH:MM:SS (whole seconds) or HH:MM:SS.sss (milliseconds) format, colons, and period required.
Valid values:
* HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-59. (without milliseconds)
* HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-59. sss=000-999 (indicating milliseconds).

LocalMktDate.name: LocalMktDate
LocalMktDate.description: Date of Local Market (vs. UTC) in YYYYMMDD format. Valid values: YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31.

UTCDate.name: UTCDate
UTCDate.description: Date represented in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") in YYYYMMDD format. Valid values: YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31.

data.name: data
data.description: Raw data with no format or content restrictions. Data fields are always immediately preceded by a length field. The length field should specify the number of bytes of the value of the data field (up to but not including the terminating SOH). Caution: the value of one of these fields may contain the delimiter (SOH) character. Note that the value specified for this field should be followed by the delimiter (SOH) character as all fields are terminated with an "SOH".

MonthYear.name: MonthYear
MonthYear.description: char field representing month of a year in YYYYMM format. Valid values: YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12.

DayOfMonth.name: DayOfMonth
DayOfMonth.description: int field representing a day during a particular monthy (values 1 to 31).

1.name: Account
1.description: Account mnemonic as agreed between broker and institution.

2.name: AdvId
2.description:
Unique identifier of advertisement message.
(Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

3.name: AdvRefID
3.description:
Reference identifier used with CANCEL and REPLACE transaction types.
(Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

6.name: AvgPx
6.description: Calculated average price of all fills on this order.

7.name: BeginSeqNo
7.description: Message sequence number of first message in range to be resent

8.name: BeginString
8.description: Identifies beginning of new message and protocol version. ALWAYS FIRST FIELD IN MESSAGE. (Always unencrypted)

9.name: BodyLength
9.description: Message length, in bytes, forward to the CheckSum field. ALWAYS SECOND FIELD IN MESSAGE. (Always unencrypted)

10.name: CheckSum
10.description: Three byte, simple checksum (see Appendix B: CheckSum Calculation for description). ALWAYS LAST FIELD IN MESSAGE; i.e. serves, with the trailing <SOH>, as the end-of-message delimiter. Always defined as three characters. (Always unencrypted)

11.name: ClOrdID
11.description: Unique identifier for Order as assigned by institution (identified by SenderCompID or OnBehalfOfCompID as appropriate). Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day. Firms, particularly those which electronically submit multi-day orders, trade globally or throughout market close periods,should ensure uniqueness across days, for example by embedding a date within the ClOrdID field.

12.name: Commission
12.description: Commission. Note if CommType is percentage, Commission of 5% should be represented as .05.

14.name: CumQty
14.description:
Total number of shares filled.
(Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

15.name: Currency
15.description: Identifies currency used for price. Absence of this field is interpreted as the default for the security. It is recommended that systems provide the currency value whenever possible. See Appendix A: Valid Currency Codes for information on obtaining valid values.

16.name: EndSeqNo
16.description: Message sequence number of last message in range to be resent. If request is for a single message BeginSeqNo = EndSeqNo. If request is for all messages subsequent to a particular message, EndSeqNo = "0" (representing infinity).

17.name: ExecID
17.description:
Unique identifier of execution message as assigned by broker (will be 0 (zero) for ExecTransType=3 (Status)).
Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day or the life of a multi-day order. Firms which accept multi-day orders should consider embedding a date within the ExecID field to assure uniqueness across days.
(Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

18.name: ExecInst
18.description: Instructions for order handling on exchange trading floor. If more than one instruction is applicable to an order, this field can contain multiple instructions separated by space.

enumValues.name: PercentOfVolume
enumValues.description: Percent of volume (indicates that the sender does not want to be all of the volume on the floor vs. a specific percentage)

enumValues.name: PrimaryPeg
enumValues.description: Primary peg (primary market - buy at bid/sell at offer)

enumValues.name: FixedPegToLocalBestBidOrOfferAtTimeOfOrder
enumValues.description: Fixed Peg to Local best bid or offer at time of order

19.name: ExecRefID
19.description:
Reference identifier used with Cancel and Correct transaction types.
(Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

21.name: HandlInst
21.description: Instructions for order handling on Broker trading floor

enumValues.name: AutomatedExecutionNoIntervention
enumValues.description: Automated execution order, private, no Broker intervention

enumValues.name: AutomatedExecutionInterventionOK
enumValues.description: Automated execution order, public, Broker intervention OK

enumValues.name: ConsolidatedTapeAssociation
enumValues.description: Consolidated Tape Association (CTA) Symbol (SIAC CTS/CQS line format)

23.name: IOIid
23.description:
Unique identifier of IOI message.
(Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

26.name: IOIRefID
26.description:
Reference identifier used with CANCEL and REPLACE, transaction types.
(Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

27.name: IOIShares
27.description: Number of shares in numeric or relative size.

30.name: LastMkt
30.description: Market of execution for last fill

31.name: LastPx
31.description: Price of this (last) fill. Field not required for ExecTransType = 3 (Status)

32.name: LastShares
32.description:
Quantity of shares bought/sold on this (last) fill. Field not required for ExecTransType = 3 (Status)
(Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

33.name: LinesOfText
33.description: Identifies number of lines of text body

35.name: MsgType
35.description:
Defines message type. ALWAYS THIRD FIELD IN MESSAGE. (Always unencrypted)
Note: A "U" as the first character in the MsgType field (i.e. U1, U2, etc) indicates that the message format is privately defined between the sender and receiver.

37.name: OrderID
37.description: Unique identifier for Order as assigned by broker. Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day. Firms which accept multi-day orders should consider embedding a date within the OrderID field to assure uniqueness across days.

38.name: OrderQty
38.description:
Number of shares ordered. This represents the number of shares for equities or based on normal convention the number of contracts for options, futures, convertible bonds, etc.
(Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

enumValues.name: PendingCancel
enumValues.description: Pending Cancel (e.g. result of Order Cancel Request)

enumValues.name: PendingReplace
enumValues.description: Pending Replace (e.g. result of Order Cancel/Replace Request)

enumValues.name: Funari
enumValues.description: Funari (Limit Day Order with unexecuted portion handled as Market On Close. e.g. Japan)

41.name: OrigClOrdID
41.description: ClOrdID of the previous order (NOT the initial order of the day) as assigned by the institution, used to identify the previous order in cancel and cancel/replace requests.

42.name: OrigTime
42.description: Time of message origination (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT"))

43.name: PossDupFlag
43.description: Indicates possible retransmission of message with this sequence number

46.name: RelatdSym
46.description: Symbol of issue related to story. Can be repeated within message to identify multiple companies.

47.name: Rule80A
47.description: Note that the name of this field is changing to "OrderCapacity" as Rule80A is a very US market-specific term. Other world markets need to convey similar information, however, often a subset of the US values. . See the "Rule80A (aka OrderCapacity) Usage by Market" appendix for market-specific usage of this field.

enumValues.name: ShortExemptTransactionAType
enumValues.description: Short exempt transaction (refer to A type)

enumValues.name: ProprietaryNonAlgo
enumValues.description: Program Order, non-index arb, for Member firm/org

enumValues.name: ProgramOrderMember
enumValues.description: Program Order, index arb, for Member firm/org

enumValues.name: ShortExemptTransactionWType
enumValues.description: Short exempt transaction (refer to W type)

enumValues.name: ShortExemptTransactionIType
enumValues.description: Short exempt transaction (refer to I type)

enumValues.name: ProprietaryAlgo
enumValues.description: Program Order, index arb, for individual customer

enumValues.name: AgencyAlgo
enumValues.description: Program Order, non-index arb, for individual customer

enumValues.name: ShortExemptTransactionMemberAffliated
enumValues.description: Short exempt transaction for member competing market-maker affiliated with the firm clearing the trade (refer to P and O types)

enumValues.name: ProgramOrderOtherMember
enumValues.description: Program Order, index arb, for other member

enumValues.name: AgentForOtherMember
enumValues.description: Program Order, non-index arb, for other member

enumValues.name: AgencyIndexArb
enumValues.description: Program Order, index arb, for other agency

enumValues.name: AllOtherOrdersAsAgentForOtherMember
enumValues.description: All other orders as agent for other member

enumValues.name: ShortExemptTransactionMemberNotAffliated
enumValues.description: Short exempt transaction for member competing market-maker not affiliated with the firm clearing the trade (refer to W and T types)

enumValues.name: AgencyNonAlgo
enumValues.description: Program Order, non-index arb, for other agency

enumValues.name: ShortExemptTransactionNonMember
enumValues.description: Short exempt transaction for non-member competing market-maker (refer to A and R types)

48.name: SecurityID
48.description: CUSIP or other alternate security identifier

49.name: SenderCompID
49.description: Assigned value used to identify firm sending message.

50.name: SenderSubID
50.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator (desk, trader, etc.)

51.name: SendingDate
51.description: No longer used. Included here for reference to prior versions.

52.name: SendingTime
52.description: Time of message transmission (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

53.name: Shares
53.description:
Number of shares
(Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

enumValues.name: Undisclosed
enumValues.description: Undisclosed (valid for IOI and List Order messages only)

enumValues.name: Cross
enumValues.description: Cross (orders where counterparty is an exchange, valid for all messages except IOIs)

56.name: TargetCompID
56.description: Assigned value used to identify receiving firm.

57.name: TargetSubID
57.description: Assigned value used to identify specific individual or unit intended to receive message. "ADMIN" reserved for administrative messages not intended for a specific user.

58.name: Text
58.description:
Free format text string
(Note: this field does not have a specified maximum length)

59.name: TimeInForce
59.description: Specifies how long the order remains in effect. Absence of this field is interpreted as DAY.

60.name: TransactTime
60.description: Time of execution/order creation (expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

62.name: ValidUntilTime
62.description: Indicates expiration time of indication message (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

63.name: SettlmntTyp
63.description: Indicates order settlement period. Absence of this field is interpreted as Regular. Regular is defined as the default settlement period for the particular security on the exchange of execution.

64.name: FutSettDate
64.description: Specific date of trade settlement (SettlementDate) in YYYYMMDD format. Required when SettlmntTyp = 6 (Future) or SettlmntTyp = 8 (Sellers Option). (expressed in local time at place of settlement)

65.name: SymbolSfx
65.description:
Additional information about the security (e.g. preferred, warrants, etc.). Note also see SecurityType.
Valid values:
As defined in the NYSE Stock and bond Symbol Directory and in the AMEX Fitch Directory

66.name: ListID
66.description: Unique identifier for list as assigned by institution, used to associate multiple individual orders. Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day. Firms which generate multi-day orders should consider embedding a date within the ListID field to assure uniqueness across days.

67.name: ListSeqNo
67.description: Sequence of individual order within list (i.e. ListSeqNo of ListNoOrds, 2 of 25, 3 of 25, . . . )

68.name: TotNoOrders
68.description:
Total number of list order entries across all messages. Should be the sum of all NoOrders in each message that has repeating list order entries related to the same ListID. Used to support fragmentation.
(Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was named "ListNoOrds")

69.name: ListExecInst
69.description: Free format text message containing list handling and execution instructions.

70.name: AllocID
70.description:
Unique identifier for allocation message.
(Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

enumValues.name: CalculatedWithoutPreliminary
enumValues.description: Calculated without Preliminary (sent unsolicited by broker, includes MiscFees and NetMoney)

72.name: RefAllocID
72.description:
Reference identifier to be used with Replace, Cancel, and Calculated AllocTransType messages.
(Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

73.name: NoOrders
73.description: Indicates number of orders to be combined for average pricing and allocation.

74.name: AvgPrxPrecision
74.description: Indicates number of decimal places to be used for average pricing. Absence of this field indicates that default precision arranged by the broker/institution is to be used.

75.name: TradeDate
75.description: Indicates date of trade referenced in this message in YYYYMMDD format. Absence of this field indicates current day (expressed in local time at place of trade).

76.name: ExecBroker
76.description: Identifies executing / give-up broker. Standard NASD market-maker mnemonic is preferred.

77.name: OpenClose
77.description: Indicates whether the resulting position after a trade should be an opening position or closing position. Used for omnibus accounting - where accounts are held on a gross basis instead of being netted together.

80.name: AllocShares
80.description:
Number of shares to be allocated to specific sub-account
(Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

81.name: ProcessCode
81.description: Processing code for sub-account. Absence of this field in AllocAccount / AllocPrice/AllocShares / ProcessCode instance indicates regular trade.

82.name: NoRpts
82.description: Total number of reports within series.

83.name: RptSeq
83.description: Sequence number of message within report series.

84.name: CxlQty
84.description:
Total number of shares canceled for this order.
(Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

85.name: NoDlvyInst
85.description:
Number of delivery instruction fields to follow
No longer used. Included here for reference to prior versions.

86.name: DlvyInst
86.description:
Free format text field to indicate delivery instructions
No longer used. Included here for reference to prior versions.

93.name: SignatureLength
93.description: Number of bytes in signature field.

95.name: RawDataLength
95.description: Number of bytes in raw data field.

96.name: RawData
96.description: Unformatted raw data, can include bitmaps, word processor documents, etc.

97.name: PossResend
97.description: Indicates that message may contain information that has been sent under another sequence number.

enumValues.name: PGPDESMD5
enumValues.description: PGP/DES-MD5 (see app note on FIX web site)

enumValues.name: PEM
enumValues.description: PEM/DES-MD5 (see app note on FIX web site)

100.name: ExDestination
100.description:
Execution destination as defined by institution when order is entered.
Valid values:
See Appendix C

102.name: CxlRejReason
102.description: Code to identify reason for cancel rejection.

enumValues.name: OrderAlreadyInPendingStatus
enumValues.description: Order already in Pending Cancel or Pending Replace status

103.name: OrdRejReason
103.description: Code to identify reason for order rejection.

enumValues.name: DuplicateOfAVerballyCommunicatedOrder
enumValues.description: Duplicate of a verbally communicated order

enumValues.name: AtTheMarket
enumValues.description: At the Market (previously called Current Quote)

105.name: WaveNo
105.description: Identifier to aid in the management of multiple lists derived from a single, master list.

106.name: Issuer
106.description: Company name of security issuer (e.g. International Business Machines)

109.name: ClientID
109.description: Firm identifier used in third party-transactions (should not be a substitute for OnBehalfOfCompID/DeliverToCompID).

110.name: MinQty
110.description:
Minimum quantity of an order to be executed.
(Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

111.name: MaxFloor
111.description:
Maximum number of shares within an order to be shown on the exchange floor at any given time.
(Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

112.name: TestReqID
112.description: Identifier included in Test Request message to be returned in resulting Heartbeat

113.name: ReportToExch
113.description: Identifies party of trade responsible for exchange reporting.

enumValues.name: SenderReports
enumValues.description: Indicates that party sending message will report trade

enumValues.name: ReceiverReports
enumValues.description: Indicates that party receiving message must report trade

114.name: LocateReqd
114.description: Indicates whether the broker is to locate the stock in conjunction with a short sell order.

enumValues.name: No
enumValues.description: Indicates the broker is not required to locate

enumValues.name: Yes
enumValues.description: Indicates the broker is responsible for locating the stock

115.name: OnBehalfOfCompID
115.description: Assigned value used to identify firm originating message if the message was delivered by a third party i.e. the third party firm identifier would be delivered in the SenderCompID field and the firm originating the message in this field.

116.name: OnBehalfOfSubID
116.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator (i.e. trader) if the message was delivered by a third party

118.name: NetMoney
118.description: Total amount due as the result of the transaction (e.g. for Buy order - principal + commission + fees) reported in currency of execution.

119.name: SettlCurrAmt
119.description: Total amount due expressed in settlement currency (includes the effect of the forex transaction)

121.name: ForexReq
121.description: Indicates request for forex accommodation trade to be executed along with security transaction.

enumValues.name: DoNotExecuteForexAfterSecurityTrade
enumValues.description: Do not execute Forex after security trade

122.name: OrigSendingTime
122.description: Original time of message transmission (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") when transmitting orders as the result of a resend request.

123.name: GapFillFlag
123.description: Indicates that the Sequence Reset message is replacing administrative or application messages which will not be resent.

enumValues.name: GapFillMessage
enumValues.description: Gap Fill message, MsgSeqNum field valid

124.name: NoExecs
124.description: No of execution repeating group entries to follow.

125.name: CxlType
125.description: No longer used. Included here for reference to prior versions.

126.name: ExpireTime
126.description: Time/Date of order expiration (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

128.name: DeliverToCompID
128.description: Assigned value used to identify the firm targeted to receive the message if the message is delivered by a third party i.e. the third party firm identifier would be delivered in the TargetCompID field and the ultimate receiver firm ID in this field.

129.name: DeliverToSubID
129.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message recipient (i.e. trader) if the message is delivered by a third party

130.name: IOINaturalFlag
130.description: Indicates that IOI is the result of an existing agency order or a facilitation position resulting from an agency order, not from principal trading or order solicitation activity.

134.name: BidSize
134.description:
Quantity of bid
(Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

135.name: OfferSize
135.description:
Quantity of offer
(Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

136.name: NoMiscFees
136.description: Number of repeating groups of miscellaneous fees

141.name: ResetSeqNumFlag
141.description: Indicates that the both sides of the FIX session should reset sequence numbers.

142.name: SenderLocationID
142.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator’s location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader)

143.name: TargetLocationID
143.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message destination’s location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader)

144.name: OnBehalfOfLocationID
144.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator’s location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader) if the message was delivered by a third party

145.name: DeliverToLocationID
145.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message recipient’s location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader) if the message was delivered by a third party

146.name: NoRelatedSym
146.description: Specifies the number of repeating symbols specified.

147.name: Subject
147.description: The subject of an Email message

148.name: Headline
148.description: The headline of a News message

149.name: URLLink
149.description: A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) link to additional information (i.e. http://www.XYZ.com/research.html)

150.name: ExecType
150.description: Describes the specific ExecutionRpt (i.e. Pending Cancel) while OrdStatus will always identify the current order status (i.e. Partially Filled)

enumValues.name: PendingCancel
enumValues.description: Pending Cancel (e.g. result of Order Cancel Request)

enumValues.name: Restated
enumValues.description: Restated (ExecutionRpt sent unsolicited by sellside, with ExecRestatementReason set)

enumValues.name: PendingReplace
enumValues.description: Pending Replace (e.g. result of Order Cancel/Replace Request)

151.name: LeavesQty
151.description:
Amount of shares open for further execution. If the OrdStatus is Canceled, DoneForTheDay, Expired, Calculated, or Rejected (in which case the order is no longer active) then LeavesQty could be 0, otherwise LeavesQty = OrderQty - CumQty.
(Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

152.name: CashOrderQty
152.description: Specifies the approximate order quantity desired in total monetary units vs. as a number of shares. The broker would be responsible for converting and calculating a share quantity (OrderQty) based upon this amount to be used for the actual order and subsequent messages.

155.name: SettlCurrFxRate
155.description: Foreign exchange rate used to compute SettlCurrAmt from Currency to SettlCurrency

156.name: SettlCurrFxRateCalc
156.description: Specifies whether or not SettlCurrFxRate should be multiplied or divided.

157.name: NumDaysInterest
157.description: Number of Days of Interest for convertible bonds and fixed income

158.name: AccruedInterestRate
158.description: Accrued Interest Rate for convertible bonds and fixed income

159.name: AccruedInterestAmt
159.description: Amount of Accrued Interest for convertible bonds and fixed income

160.name: SettlInstMode
160.description: Indicates mode used for Settlement Instructions

161.name: AllocText
161.description: Free format text related to a specific AllocAccount.

162.name: SettlInstID
162.description: Unique identifier for Settlement Instructions message.

164.name: EmailThreadID
164.description: Unique identifier for an email thread (new and chain of replies)

166.name: SettlLocation
166.description: Identifies Settlement Depository or Country Code (ISITC spec)

enumValues.name: Wildcard
enumValues.description: Wildcard entry (used on Security Definition Request message)

enumValues.name: ConvertibleBond
enumValues.description: Convertible Bond (Note not part of ISITC spec)

enumValues.name: CatsTigersAndLions
enumValues.description: Cats, Tigers & Lions (a real code: US Treasury Receipts)

168.name: EffectiveTime
168.description: Time the details within the message should take effect (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

169.name: StandInstDbType
169.description: Identifies the Standing Instruction database used

enumValues.name: AGlobalCustodian
enumValues.description: A Global Custodian (StandInstDbName must be provided)

170.name: StandInstDbName
170.description: Name of the Standing Instruction database represented with StandInstDbType (i.e. the Global Custodian’s name).

171.name: StandInstDbID
171.description: Unique identifier used on the Standing Instructions database for the Standing Instructions to be referenced.

173.name: SettlDepositoryCode
173.description: Broker’s account code at the depository (i.e. CEDEL ID for CEDEL, FINS for DTC, or Euroclear ID for Euroclear) if SettlLocation is a depository

174.name: SettlBrkrCode
174.description: BIC (Bank Identification Code—Swift managed) code of the broker involved (i.e. for multi-company brokerage firms)

175.name: SettlInstCode
175.description: BIC (Bank Identification Code—Swift managed) code of the institution involved (i.e. for multi-company institution firms)

176.name: SecuritySettlAgentName
176.description: Name of SettlInstSource's local agent bank if SettlLocation is not a depository

177.name: SecuritySettlAgentCode
177.description: BIC (Bank Identification Code--Swift managed) code of the SettlInstSource's local agent bank if SettlLocation is not a depository

178.name: SecuritySettlAgentAcctNum
178.description: SettlInstSource's account number at local agent bank if SettlLocation is not a depository

179.name: SecuritySettlAgentAcctName
179.description: Name of SettlInstSource's account at local agent bank if SettlLocation is not a depository

180.name: SecuritySettlAgentContactName
180.description: Name of contact at local agent bank for SettlInstSource's account if SettlLocation is not a depository

181.name: SecuritySettlAgentContactPhone
181.description: Phone number for contact at local agent bank if SettlLocation is not a depository

182.name: CashSettlAgentName
182.description: Name of SettlInstSource's local agent bank if SettlDeliveryType=Free

183.name: CashSettlAgentCode
183.description: BIC (Bank Identification Code--Swift managed) code of the SettlInstSource's local agent bank if SettlDeliveryType=Free

184.name: CashSettlAgentAcctNum
184.description: SettlInstSource's account number at local agent bank if SettlDeliveryType=Free

185.name: CashSettlAgentAcctName
185.description: Name of SettlInstSource's account at local agent bank if SettlDeliveryType=Free

186.name: CashSettlAgentContactName
186.description: Name of contact at local agent bank for SettlInstSource's account if SettlDeliveryType=Free

187.name: CashSettlAgentContactPhone
187.description: Phone number for contact at local agent bank for SettlInstSource's account if SettlDeliveryType=Free

188.name: BidSpotRate
188.description: Bid F/X spot rate.y vary and not limited to four)

189.name: BidForwardPoints
189.description: Bid F/X forward points added to spot rate. May be a negative value.

191.name: OfferForwardPoints
191.description: Offer F/X forward points added to spot rate. May be a negative value.

192.name: OrderQty2
192.description: OrderQty of the future part of a F/X swap order.

193.name: FutSettDate2
193.description: FutSettDate of the future part of a F/X swap order.

195.name: LastForwardPoints
195.description: F/X forward points added to LastSpotRate. May be a negative value.

196.name: AllocLinkID
196.description: Can be used to link two different Allocation messages (each with unique AllocID) together, i.e. for F/X "Netting" or "Swaps". Should be unique.

197.name: AllocLinkType
197.description: Identifies the type of Allocation linkage when AllocLinkID is used.

198.name: SecondaryOrderID
198.description: Assigned by the party which accepts the order. Can be used to provide the OrderID used by an exchange or executing system.

199.name: NoIOIQualifiers
199.description: Number of repeating groups of IOIQualifiers.

200.name: MaturityMonthYear
200.description:
Month and Year of the maturity for SecurityType=FUT or SecurityType=OPT. Required if MaturityDay is specified.
Format: YYYYMM
(i.e. 199903)

201.name: PutOrCall
201.description: Indicates whether an Option is for a put or call.

204.name: CustomerOrFirm
204.description: Used for options when delivering the order to an execution system/exchange to specify if the order is for a customer or the firm placing the order itself.

205.name: MaturityDay
205.description: Day of month used in conjunction with MaturityMonthYear to specify the maturity date for SecurityType=FUT or SecurityType=OPT.

206.name: OptAttribute
206.description: Can be used for SecurityType=OPT to identify a particular security.

207.name: SecurityExchange
207.description: Market used to help identify a security.

208.name: NotifyBrokerOfCredit
208.description: Indicates whether or not details should be communicated to BrokerOfCredit (i.e. step-in broker).

209.name: AllocHandlInst
209.description: Indicates how the receiver (i.e. third party) of Allocation message should handle/process the account details.

210.name: MaxShow
210.description:
Maximum number of shares within an order to be shown to other customers (i.e. sent via an IOI).
(Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

211.name: PegDifference
211.description: Amount (signed) added to the price of the peg for a pegged order.

212.name: XmlDataLen
212.description: Length of the XmlData data block.

213.name: XmlData
213.description: Actual XML data stream (e.g. FIXML). See approriate XML reference (e.g. FIXML). Note: may contain embedded SOH characters.

214.name: SettlInstRefID
214.description: Reference identifier for the SettlInstID with Cancel and Replace SettlInstTransType transaction types.

215.name: NoRoutingIDs
215.description:
Number of repeating groups of RoutingID and RoutingType values.
See Appendix L – Pre-Trade Message Targeting/Routing

216.name: RoutingType
216.description: Indicates the type of RoutingID specified.

217.name: RoutingID
217.description: Assigned value used to identify a specific routing destination.

218.name: SpreadToBenchmark
218.description: For Fixed Income. Basis points relative to a benchmark. To be expressed as "count of basis points" (vs. an absolute value). E.g. High Grade Corporate Bonds may express price as basis points relative to benchmark (the Benchmark field). Note: Basis points can be negative.

219.name: Benchmark
219.description: For Fixed Income. Identifies the benchmark (e.g. used in conjunction with the SpreadToBenchmark field).

223.name: CouponRate
223.description: For Fixed Income. Coupon rate of the bond. Will be zero for step-up bonds.

231.name: ContractMultiplier
231.description: Specifies the ratio or multiply factor to convert from contracts to shares (e.g. 1.0, 100, 1000, etc). Applicable For Fixed Income, Convertible Bonds, Derivatives, etc. Note: If used, quantities should be expressed in the "nominal" (e.g. contracts vs. shares) amount.

262.name: MDReqID
262.description: Unique identifier for Market Data Request

264.name: MarketDepth
264.description: Depth of market for Book Snapshot

265.name: MDUpdateType
265.description: Specifies the type of Market Data update.

266.name: AggregatedBook
266.description: Specifies whether or not book entries should be aggregated.

enumValues.name: BookEntriesShouldNotBeAggregated
enumValues.description: Multiple entries per side per price allowed

enumValues.name: BookEntriesToBeAggregated
enumValues.description: one book entry per side per price

268.name: NoMDEntries
268.description: Number of entries in Market Data message.

270.name: MDEntryPx
270.description: Price of the Market Data Entry.

271.name: MDEntrySize
271.description: Number of shares represented by the Market Data Entry.

275.name: MDMkt
275.description:
Market posting quote / trade.
Valid values:
See Appendix C

276.name: QuoteCondition
276.description: Space-delimited list of conditions describing a quote.

277.name: TradeCondition
277.description: Space-delimited list of conditions describing a trade

enumValues.name: StoppedStock
enumValues.description: Stopped Stock (guarantee of price but does not execute the order)

279.name: MDUpdateAction
279.description: Type of Market Data update action.

281.name: MDReqRejReason
281.description: Reason for the rejection of a Market Data request.

282.name: MDEntryOriginator
282.description: Originator of a Market Data Entry

283.name: LocationID
283.description: Identification of a Market Maker’s location

284.name: DeskID
284.description: Identification of a Market Maker’s desk

287.name: SellerDays
287.description: Specifies the number of days that may elapse before delivery of the security

290.name: MDEntryPositionNo
290.description: Display position of a bid or offer, numbered from most competitive to least competitive, per market side, beginning with 1.

292.name: CorporateAction
292.description: Identifies the type of Corporate Action.

295.name: NoQuoteEntries
295.description: The number of quote entries for a QuoteSet.

296.name: NoQuoteSets
296.description: The number of sets of quotes in the message.

297.name: QuoteAckStatus
297.description: Identifies the status of the quote acknowledgement.

298.name: QuoteCancelType
298.description: Identifies the type of quote cancel.

299.name: QuoteEntryID
299.description: Uniquely identifies the quote as part of a QuoteSet.

301.name: QuoteResponseLevel
301.description: Level of Response requested from receiver of quote messages.

enumValues.name: AcknowledgeOnlyNegativeOrErroneousQuotes
enumValues.description: Acknowledge only negative or erroneous quotes

302.name: QuoteSetID
302.description: Unique id for the Quote Set.

303.name: QuoteRequestType
303.description: Indicates the type of Quote Request being generated

304.name: TotQuoteEntries
304.description: Total number of quotes for the quote set across all messages. Should be the sum of all NoQuoteEntries in each message that has repeating quotes that are part of the same quote set.

306.name: UnderlyingIssuer
306.description:
Underlying security’s Issuer.
See Issuer field for description

307.name: UnderlyingSecurityDesc
307.description:
Underlying security’s SecurityDesc.
See SecurityDesc field for description

308.name: UnderlyingSecurityExchange
308.description: Underlying security’s SecurityExchange. Can be used to identify the underlying security.

309.name: UnderlyingSecurityID
309.description:
Underlying security’s SecurityID.
See SecurityID field for description

311.name: UnderlyingSymbol
311.description:
Underlying security’s Symbol.
See Symbol field for description

312.name: UnderlyingSymbolSfx
312.description:
Underlying security’s SymbolSfx.
See SymbolSfx field for description

313.name: UnderlyingMaturityMonthYear
313.description:
Underlying security’s MaturityMonthYear. Required if UnderlyingMaturityDay is specified.
See MaturityMonthYear field for description

314.name: UnderlyingMaturityDay
314.description:
Underlying security’s MaturityDay.
See MaturityDay field for description

315.name: UnderlyingPutOrCall
315.description:
Underlying security’s PutOrCall.
See PutOrCall field for description

316.name: UnderlyingStrikePrice
316.description:
Underlying security’s StrikePrice.
See StrikePrice field for description

317.name: UnderlyingOptAttribute
317.description:
Underlying security’s OptAttribute.
See OptAttribute field for description

318.name: UnderlyingCurrency
318.description:
Underlying security’s Currency.
See Currency field for description and valid values

319.name: RatioQty
319.description: Quantity of a particular leg in the security.

320.name: SecurityReqID
320.description: Unique ID of a Security Definition Request.

enumValues.name: RequestSecurityIdentityForSpecifications
enumValues.description: Request Security identity for the specifications provided (Name of the security is not supplied)

enumValues.name: RequestListSecurities
enumValues.description: Request List Securities (Can be qualified with Symbol, SecurityType, TradingSessionID, SecurityExchange is provided then only list Securities for the specific type)

322.name: SecurityResponseID
322.description: Unique ID of a Security Definition message.

323.name: SecurityResponseType
323.description: Type of Security Definition message response.

enumValues.name: AcceptWithRevisions
enumValues.description: Accept security proposal with revisions as indicated in the message

enumValues.name: ListOfSecurityTypesReturnedPerRequest
enumValues.description: List of security types returned per request

enumValues.name: ListOfSecuritiesReturnedPerRequest
enumValues.description: List of securities returned per request

324.name: SecurityStatusReqID
324.description: Unique ID of a Security Status Request message.

325.name: UnsolicitedIndicator
325.description: Indicates whether or not message is being sent as a result of a subscription request or not.

enumValues.name: MessageIsBeingSentAsAResultOfAPriorRequest
enumValues.description: Message is being sent as a result of a prior request

326.name: SecurityTradingStatus
326.description: Identifies the trading status applicable to the transaction.

enumValues.name: NotAvailableForTrading
enumValues.description: Not Available for trading (end of session)

327.name: HaltReason
327.description: Denotes the reason for the Opening Delay or Trading Halt.

328.name: InViewOfCommon
328.description: Indicates whether or not the halt was due to Common Stock trading being halted.

enumValues.name: HaltWasNotRelatedToAHaltOfTheCommonStock
enumValues.description: Halt was not related to a halt of the common stock

enumValues.name: HaltWasDueToCommonStockBeingHalted
enumValues.description: Halt was due to common stock being halted

329.name: DueToRelated
329.description: Indicates whether or not the halt was due to the Related Security being halted.

enumValues.name: NotRelatedToSecurityHalt
enumValues.description: Halt was not related to a halt of the related security

enumValues.name: RelatedToSecurityHalt
enumValues.description: Halt was due to related security being halted

332.name: HighPx
332.description: Represents an indication of the high end of the price range for a security prior to the open or reopen

333.name: LowPx
333.description: Represents an indication of the low end of the price range for a security prior to the open or reopen

335.name: TradSesReqID
335.description: Unique ID of a Trading Session Status message.

336.name: TradingSessionID
336.description:
Identifier for Trading Session
Can be used to represent a specific market trading session (e.g. "PRE-OPEN", "CROSS_2", "AFTER-HOURS", "TOSTNET1", "TOSTNET2", etc).
Values should be bi-laterally agreed to between counterparties.

337.name: ContraTrader
337.description: Identifies the trader (e.g. "badge number") of the ContraBroker.

341.name: TradSesStartTime
341.description: Starting time of the trading session

342.name: TradSesOpenTime
342.description: Time of the opening of the trading session

343.name: TradSesPreCloseTime
343.description: Time of the pre-closed of the trading session

344.name: TradSesCloseTime
344.description: Closing time of the trading session

345.name: TradSesEndTime
345.description: End time of the trading session

346.name: NumberOfOrders
346.description: Number of orders in the market.

347.name: MessageEncoding
347.description: Type of message encoding (non-ASCII (non-English) characters) used in a message’s "Encoded" fields.

348.name: EncodedIssuerLen
348.description: Byte length of encoded (non-ASCII characters) EncodedIssuer field.

349.name: EncodedIssuer
349.description: Encoded (non-ASCII characters) representation of the Issuer field in the encoded format specified via the MessageEncoding field. If used, the ASCII (English) representation should also be specified in the Issuer field.

350.name: EncodedSecurityDescLen
350.description: Byte length of encoded (non-ASCII characters) EncodedSecurityDesc field.

351.name: EncodedSecurityDesc
351.description: Encoded (non-ASCII characters) representation of the SecurityDesc field in the encoded format specified via the MessageEncoding field. If used, the ASCII (English) representation should also be specified in the SecurityDesc field.

352.name: EncodedListExecInstLen
352.description: Byte length of encoded (non-ASCII characters) EncodedListExecInst field.

353.name: EncodedListExecInst
353.description: Encoded (non-ASCII characters) representation of the ListExecInst field in the encoded format specified via the MessageEncoding field. If used, the ASCII (English) representation should also be specified in the ListExecInst field.

354.name: EncodedTextLen
354.description: Byte length of encoded (non-ASCII characters) EncodedText field.

355.name: EncodedText
355.description: Encoded (non-ASCII characters) representation of the Text field in the encoded format specified via the MessageEncoding field. If used, the ASCII (English) representation should also be specified in the Text field.

356.name: EncodedSubjectLen
356.description: Byte length of encoded (non-ASCII characters) EncodedSubject field.

357.name: EncodedSubject
357.description: Encoded (non-ASCII characters) representation of the Subject field in the encoded format specified via the MessageEncoding field. If used, the ASCII (English) representation should also be specified in the Subject field.

358.name: EncodedHeadlineLen
358.description: Byte length of encoded (non-ASCII characters) EncodedHeadline field.

359.name: EncodedHeadline
359.description: Encoded (non-ASCII characters) representation of the Headline field in the encoded format specified via the MessageEncoding field. If used, the ASCII (English) representation should also be specified in the Headline field.

360.name: EncodedAllocTextLen
360.description: Byte length of encoded (non-ASCII characters) EncodedAllocText field.

361.name: EncodedAllocText
361.description: Encoded (non-ASCII characters) representation of the AllocText field in the encoded format specified via the MessageEncoding field. If used, the ASCII (English) representation should also be specified in the AllocText field.

362.name: EncodedUnderlyingIssuerLen
362.description: Byte length of encoded (non-ASCII characters) EncodedUnderlyingIssuer field.

363.name: EncodedUnderlyingIssuer
363.description: Encoded (non-ASCII characters) representation of the UnderlyingIssuer field in the encoded format specified via the MessageEncoding field. If used, the ASCII (English) representation should also be specified in the UnderlyingIssuer field.

364.name: EncodedUnderlyingSecurityDescLen
364.description: Byte length of encoded (non-ASCII characters) EncodedUnderlyingSecurityDesc field.

365.name: EncodedUnderlyingSecurityDesc
365.description: Encoded (non-ASCII characters) representation of the UnderlyingSecurityDesc field in the encoded format specified via the MessageEncoding field. If used, the ASCII (English) representation should also be specified in the UnderlyingSecurityeDesc field.

366.name: AllocPrice
366.description: Executed price for an AllocAccount entry used when using "executed price" vs. "average price" allocations (e.g. Japan).

367.name: QuoteSetValidUntilTime
367.description: Indicates expiration time of this particular QuoteSet (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

369.name: LastMsgSeqNumProcessed
369.description: The last MsgSeqNum value received and processed. Can be specified on every message sent. Useful for detecting a backlog with a counterparty.

370.name: OnBehalfOfSendingTime
370.description: Used when a message is sent via a "hub" or "service bureau". If A sends to Q (the hub) who then sends to B via a separate FIX session, then when Q sends to B the value of this field should represent the SendingTime on the message A sent to Q. (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

371.name: RefTagID
371.description: The tag number of the FIX field being referenced.

372.name: RefMsgType
372.description: The MsgType of the FIX message being referenced.

373.name: SessionRejectReason
373.description: Code to identify reason for a session-level Reject message.

enumValues.name: TagNotDefinedForThisMessageType
enumValues.description: Tag not defined for this message type

enumValues.name: ValueIsIncorrect
enumValues.description: Value is incorrect (out of range) for this tag

374.name: BidRequestTransType
374.description: Identifies the Bid Request message type.

375.name: ContraBroker
375.description: Identifies contra broker. Standard NASD market-maker mnemonic is preferred.

376.name: ComplianceID
376.description: ID used to represent this transaction for compliance purposes (e.g. OATS reporting).

377.name: SolicitedFlag
377.description: Indicates whether or not the order was solicited.

378.name: ExecRestatementReason
378.description: Code to identify reason for an ExecutionRpt message sent with ExecType=Restated or used when communicating an unsolicited cancel.

enumValues.name: PartialDeclineOfOrderQty
enumValues.description: Partial decline of OrderQty (e.g. exchange-initiated partial cancel)

379.name: BusinessRejectRefID
379.description: The value of the business-level "ID" field on the message being referenced.

380.name: BusinessRejectReason
380.description: Code to identify reason for a Business Message Reject message.

381.name: GrossTradeAmt
381.description: Total amount traded (e.g. CumQty * AvgPx) expressed in units of currency.

383.name: MaxMessageSize
383.description: Maximum number of bytes supported for a single message.

384.name: NoMsgTypes
384.description: Number of MsgTypes in repeating group.

385.name: MsgDirection
385.description: Specifies the direction of the messsage.

386.name: NoTradingSessions
386.description: Number of TradingSessionIDs in repeating group.

388.name: DiscretionInst
388.description: Code to identify the price a DiscretionOffset is related to and should be mathematically added to.

389.name: DiscretionOffset
389.description: Amount (signed) added to the "related to" price specified via DiscretionInst.

390.name: BidID
390.description: Unique identifier for Bid Response as assigned by broker. Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day.

391.name: ClientBidID
391.description: Unique identifier for a Bid Request as assigned by institution. Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day.

394.name: BidType
394.description: Code to identify the type of Bid Request.

399.name: BidDescriptorType
399.description: Code to identify the type of BidDescriptor.

400.name: BidDescriptor
400.description: BidDescriptor value. Usage depends upon BidDescriptorType.

401.name: SideValueInd
401.description: Code to identify which "SideValue" the value refers to. SideValue1 and SideValue2 are used as opposed to Buy or Sell so that the basket can be quoted either way as Buy or Sell.

402.name: LiquidityPctLow
402.description: Liquidity indicator or lower limit if TotalNumSecurities > 1. Represented as a percentage.

403.name: LiquidityPctHigh
403.description: Upper liquidity indicator if TotalNumSecurities > 1. Represented as a percentage.

404.name: LiquidityValue
404.description: Value between LiquidityPctLow and LiquidityPctHigh in Currency

405.name: EFPTrackingError
405.description: Eg Used in EFP trades 12% (EFP – Exchange for Physical ). Represented as a percentage.

407.name: OutsideIndexPct
407.description: Used in EFP trades. Represented as a percentage.

409.name: LiquidityIndType
409.description: Code to identify the type of liquidity indicator.

410.name: WtAverageLiquidity
410.description: Overall weighted average liquidity expressed as a % of average daily volume. Represented as a percentage.

411.name: ExchangeForPhysical
411.description: Indicates whether or not to exchange for phsyical.

413.name: CrossPercent
413.description: Percentage of program that crosses in Currency. Represented as a percentage.

414.name: ProgRptReqs
414.description: Code to identify the desired frequency of progress reports.

enumValues.name: BuySideRequests
enumValues.description: BuySide explicitly requests status using StatusRequest (Default) The sell-side firm can however, send a DONE status List Status Response in an unsolicited fashion

enumValues.name: SellSideSends
enumValues.description: SellSide periodically sends status using ListStatus. Period optionally specified in ProgressPeriod

415.name: ProgPeriodInterval
415.description: Time in minutes between each ListStatus report sent by SellSide. Zero means don’t send status.

416.name: IncTaxInd
416.description: Code to represent whether value is net (inclusive of tax) or gross.

417.name: NumBidders
417.description: Indicates the total number of bidders on the list

418.name: TradeType
418.description: Code to represent the type of trade.

419.name: BasisPxType
419.description: Code to represent the basis price type.

enumValues.name: VWAPThroughADayExcept
enumValues.description: VWAP through a day except YORI

enumValues.name: VWAPThroughAMorningSessionExcept
enumValues.description: VWAP through a morning session except YORI

enumValues.name: VWAPThroughAnAfternoonSessionExcept
enumValues.description: VWAP through an afternoon session except YORI

420.name: NoBidComponents
420.description: Indicates the number of list entries.

422.name: TotNoStrikes
422.description: Total number of strike price entries across all messages. Should be the sum of all NoStrikes in each message that has repeating strike price entries related to the same ListID. Used to support fragmentation.

423.name: PriceType
423.description: Code to represent the price type.

424.name: DayOrderQty
424.description: For GT orders, the OrderQty less all shares (adjusted for stock splits) that traded on previous days. DayOrderQty = OrderQty – (CumQty - DayCumQty)

425.name: DayCumQty
425.description: The number of shares on a GT order that have traded today.

426.name: DayAvgPx
426.description: The average price of shares on a GT order that have traded today.

427.name: GTBookingInst
427.description: Code to identify whether to book out executions on a part-filled GT order on the day of execution or to accumulate.

enumValues.name: BookOutAllTradesOnDayOfExecution
enumValues.description: book out all trades on day of execution

enumValues.name: AccumulateUntilFilledOrExpired
enumValues.description: accumulate executions until order is filled or expires

428.name: NoStrikes
428.description: Number of list strike price entries.

429.name: ListStatusType
429.description: Code to represent the price type.

430.name: NetGrossInd
430.description: Code to represent whether value is net (inclusive of tax) or gross.

431.name: ListOrderStatus
431.description: Code to represent the status of a list order.

432.name: ExpireDate
432.description: Date of order expiration (last day the order can trade), always expressed in terms of the local market date. The time at which the order expires is determined by the local market’s business practices

enumValues.name: WaitForInstruction
enumValues.description: Wait for Execute Instruction (e.g. a List Execute message or phone call before proceeding with execution of the list)

434.name: CxlRejResponseTo
434.description: Identifies the type of request that a Cancel Reject is in response to.

435.name: UnderlyingCouponRate
435.description:
Underlying security’s CouponRate.
See CouponRate field for description

436.name: UnderlyingContractMultiplier
436.description:
Underlying security’s ContractMultiplier.
See ContractMultiplier field for description

438.name: ContraTradeTime
438.description: Identifes the time of the trade with the ContraBroker. (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

439.name: ClearingFirm
439.description: Firm that will clear the trade. Used if different from the executing firm.

440.name: ClearingAccount
440.description: Supplemental accounting information forwared to clearing house/firm.

441.name: LiquidityNumSecurities
441.description: Number of Securites between LiquidityPctLow and LiquidityPctHigh in Currency.

442.name: MultiLegReportingType
442.description: Used to indicate what an Execution Report represents (e.g. used with multi-leg securiteis, such as option strategies, spreads, etc.).

enumValues.name: IndividualLegOfAMultiLegSecurity
enumValues.description: Individual leg of a multi-leg security

443.name: StrikeTime
443.description: The time at which current market prices are used to determine the value of a basket.

444.name: ListStatusText
444.description: Free format text string related to List Status.

445.name: EncodedListStatusTextLen
445.description: Byte length of encoded (non-ASCII characters) EncodedListStatusText field.

446.name: EncodedListStatusText
446.description: Encoded (non-ASCII characters) representation of the ListStatusText field in the encoded format specified via the MessageEncoding field. If used, the ASCII (English) representation should also be specified in the ListStatusText field.

coverageGaps.detail: Referenced by fields but absent from Datatypes.xml; synthesized base "String".
Referenced by fields but absent from Datatypes.xml; synthesized base "int".
